member.name: Ben Lake

addresses: Bryndulais, 67 Bridge Street, Lampeter, Ceredigion, SA48 7AB

member.synopsis: Ben Lake is the Plaid Cymru MP for Ceredigion Preseli , and has been an MP continually since 8 June 2017.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Radio Frequencies: Fees and Charges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, whether she has had discussions with HM Treasury on alternative uses of spectrum-related revenues to support national connectivity and resilience objectives.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The resilience of the UK’s critical national infrastructure is of central importance to the essential services the public rely on. We welcome Ofcom’s review of the power resilience of mobile networks, recognising the complexity of the challenge but also t...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Television Licences: Fees and Charges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what analysis her Department has conducted on the potential benefits and impacts of redirecting a portion of Annual Licence Fee revenues towards resilience-enhancing measures.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The resilience of the UK’s critical national infrastructure is of central importance to the essential services the public rely on. We welcome Ofcom’s review of the power resilience of mobile networks, recognising the complexity of the challenge but also t...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Telecommunications: Energy
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, Technology what discussions she has had with the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero and DNOs on ensuring rapid restoration of power to telecommunications sites during major inc
writtenQuestions.answerText: My department works very closely with DESNZ on shared resilience issues. The Government aims to promote collaboration between the electricity and telecommunications sectors, so that when power cuts occur the risk of disruption to services is reduced and a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Prostate Cancer: Screening
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether the UK National Screening Committee plans to build use of Stockholm3 into its screening model.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK National Screening Committee (UK NSC) has committed to examine any significant peer reviewed evidence that is relevant to its recommendation on prostate cancer screening, including reviewing evidence on biomarkers such as Stockholm3.When new peer r...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Hospitality Industry: VAT
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what recent assessment has she made of the potential merits of reducing VAT to 10% for hospitality businesses in Wales.
writtenQuestions.answerText: VAT is a broad-based tax on consumption. It is a reserved tax and applies across the UK, with the 20 per cent standard rate applying to most goods and services. Reduced rates of VAT come at a significant cost to the Exchequer and reduce the revenue availa...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Imports: VAT
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, if she will commission the Office for Budget Responsibility to undertake an independent assessment of the fiscal impact of (a) withdrawing postponed VAT accounting for imports and (b) requiring payment of import VAT
writtenQuestions.answerText: Postponed VAT accounting provides significant support for businesses, helping to manage cash flow and facilitate imports. HMRC undertakes regular operational work to ensure compliance with the rules around postponed VAT accounting. The VAT gap has reduced...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Imports: VAT
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, pursuant to the Answer of 20 May 2025 to Question 51908, whether she has made an estimate of (a) the cash-flow to the Exchequer of withdrawing postponed VAT accounting for imports, including the interest foregone on
writtenQuestions.answerText: Postponed VAT accounting provides significant support for businesses, helping to manage cash flow and facilitate imports. HMRC undertakes regular operational work to ensure compliance with the rules around postponed VAT accounting. The VAT gap has reduced...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Probate: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what steps he is taking to reduce the time taken to issue a grant of probate.
writtenQuestions.answerText: HM Courts & Tribunals Service has invested in more staff in 2026, alongside system and process improvements and a programme of upskilling to improve the processing time for applications and in order to ensure the outstanding caseload does not increase...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Hannah Schafer
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what steps she is taking with international partners to help secure the release of Hannah Schafer.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to my statement to the House on 21 May, and to the answer provided on 1 June in response to Question 2817.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Mobile Phones: Aerials
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what proportion of mobile phone mast sites servicing rural areas have alternative power generation or a battery back-up that can be accessed during an unplanned power outage.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Government does not hold this level of detailed information. Mobile network operators have legal obligations to put in place appropriate and proportionate measures to ensure the resilience of their networks and services. This is overseen by the independen...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Power Failures
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what was the average duration of an unplanned power outage in each Distribution Network Operator area in each of the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK has a highly resilient energy network, and industry works to continuously minimise the risk and impacts of unplanned outages. All DNOs are regulated by the independent regulator, Ofgem, and must report their performance against annual targets to Of...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Power Failures
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, how many unplanned power outages occurred in each of the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK has a highly resilient energy network, and industry works to continuously minimise the risk and impacts of unplanned outages. All DNOs are regulated by the independent regulator, Ofgem, and must report their performance against annual targets to Of...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Marriage Act 1949: Reform
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, when the consultation on the Marriage Act 1949 reforms will be launched.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has announced its intention to reform weddings law when parliamentary time allows.The reforms reflect a commitment to making marriage law fairer, simpler, and more modern, whilst also protecting the solemnity and dignity of marriage. The cu...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Seeds: Import Controls
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether she has considered the potential impact of the re-introduction of the Places of Destination scheme on the home garden seed sector.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There are no plans to re-introduce the Place of Destination Scheme and therefore there been no assessment of the impact its re-introduction would have on the home garden seed sector.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Seeds: Imports
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what estimate her Department has made of the volume of seed imported into the UK via online marketplaces in each year since 2020.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Seed import applications do not capture data on how these imports are sourced.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Seeds: Imports
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what the amount of seeds imported into the UK from EU countries was by weight in each year since 2020.

writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Seeds: Imports
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what the amount of seeds imported into the UK from non-EU countries was by weight in each year since 2020.

writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Mobile Phones: Standards

writtenQuestions.answerText: Landlines are not being removed. The technology underpinning the landline network, the Public Switched Telephone Network (PSTN), is being upgraded to digital Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP).As per the response to Question 128431 on 28 April, I can rei...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Mobile Phones: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, with reference to the answer of 28 April 2026 to question 128431, what her Department considers to be an adequate landline alternative.

writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury
